films.title: Merrily We Roll Along
films.review: never really seen anything like this, tremendously written. direction of the production seems great — has this propulsive energy from start to finish — but the proshot is a bit tough. needed a lot more wides

films.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
films.review: barely a movie, but just the exact nostalgia sweet spot for me

films.title: Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie
If I Had Legs I'd Kick You
The Testament of Ann Lee
films.review:
great, generous (but open eyed) look at religious ecstasy and the insular narratives that tend to surround it.

so many walkouts of this one. also unfortunately really poor audio at the screening, suspect i’d like it even more with a better projection

films.title: Hamnet
films.review: Loved the first act, absolutely wept through the second, totally numb to the third. Found so much of the third act plot to be so rickety that I never caught the wave leading to the catharsis so many others seem to have found.

films.title: A House of Dynamite
films.review: really gripping to start but steadily wanes. just completely falls apart in the 3rd act when it becomes west wing, but worse.

films.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
films.review:
:( 

Lots wrong with this one, but maybe the weirdest is how generically placeless it all is. Columbus & After Yang both were at their best with the specific power of home/heritage. Sad to see it missing here

films.title: Materialists
films.review: interesting, glossy, maybe evil? dakota johnson innocent

films.title: Next to Normal
films.review:
was wondering what the “born to be your lover, cursed to be your mother” energy was about in the first couple scenes

at times flirts with transcendence with its score and sweeping emotion. nearly collapses under the sheer weight of the theater kid energy at the end (say his name, lol)

films.title: The Personal History of David Copperfield
The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh
Aladdin
films.review: ellis absolutely losing his mind every minute genie is on screen 💙 movie zips at lightning pace - they don’t make ‘em like this anymore

films.title: Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace
